summary: Owns the formal quality system, governs CQI rigor, and ensures compliance, traceability, and controlled change.
responsibilities: Define and maintain quality metrics, control limits, and capability baselines
Oversee the Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) governance framework
Ensure compliance with regulatory, safety, and organizational standards
Conduct audits, manage audit schedules, and verify corrective actions
Review and approve changes to controlled documents and standard work
Manage the human-review queue for ambiguous or high-risk deviations
Validate improvement outcomes using statistical and evidence-based methods
Ensure proper documentation, traceability, and version control for all process changes
Collaborate with Process Engineers to evaluate systemic risks and improvement opportunities
Provide quality training and reinforce quality culture across the organization
authorities: Define controlled parameters and attribute authority
Initiate formal investigations into quality or safety issues
Suspend operations in case of critical quality failures
decisionRights: Definition of quality standards and thresholds
kaizenCapabilities: Identifying waste related to rework, defects, and variation

constraints: All decisions must comply with regulatory and organizational standards
Changes must be documented and version-controlled
